analysis: Across the top heuristics the pipeline is consistent: (1) Pareto/nondominated prefilter, (2) cheap proxy scoring (box-volume ± weight‑vector scalarizations) to build an adaptive shortlist, (3) a greedy/CELF lazy‑greedy stage using cached singleton (and sometimes pairwise) hypervolumes, and (4) bounded local 1‑for‑1 swap refinement and deterministic padding. Stronger variants reduce expensive HV calls via smaller shortlists, CELF timestamping, singleton/pairwise caches, box‑volume upper bounds, and explicit HV‑call budgets. Weaker ones either (a) do expensive full HV recomputations in inner loops, (b) lack tight pruning or caching, or (c) overuse large shortlists or uncontrolled swaps. Common avoidable overheads: repeated vstack/array reconstruction, Python-level "in" checks inside hot loops, and naïve recomputation of full-set HV instead of incremental or cached deltas. Pairwise caching is worthwhile only when shortlist L is small (L^2 affordable). Approximate HVC via moderate weight vectors (or Dirichlet samples) is a good cheap filter; many more weights improve accuracy but cost CPU. Determinism, small adaptive shortlist proportional to k and pareto size, CELF lazy updates, singleton/pairwise caches, box‑upper bounds and an HV budget produce the best tradeoffs between quality and runtime.
experience: Use CELF with cached singletons + selective pairwise HVs, build an adaptive shortlist via moderate-weight scalar HVC and box‑upper bounds, enforce an HV‑call budget, vectorize and reuse buffers, limit targeted swaps, and prefer deterministic seeds. Consider incremental HV updates or parallel HV evaluations to boost exact checks.
current_reflection: Use CELF with cached singletons + selective pairwise HVs, build an adaptive shortlist via moderate-weight scalar HVC and box‑upper bounds, enforce an HV‑call budget, vectorize and reuse buffers, limit targeted swaps, and prefer deterministic seeds. Consider incremental HV updates or parallel HV evaluations to boost exact checks.
good_reflections: Prefilter dominated points, approximate HVC with few weight vectors to shortlist candidates, then perform a local search using cached incremental HVC deltas. Minimize Python-side HV calls by batching and avoid nested HV recomputations. Make the algorithm deterministic (seeded) and ensure it always returns exactly k points.
bad_reflections: Prefer a compact k‑proportional shortlist scored by vectorized box+multiweight scalarization, cache singleton HVs, run CELF lazy greedy with cheap upper‑bound pruning, then do limited deterministic 1‑for‑1 swaps. Vectorize nondominated filtering, avoid nested Python loops, only pairwise-cache when shortlist is small, and instrument an HV‑call budget. This yields strong hypervolume with predictable runtime.

Make next‑gen heuristics: (1) rank by cheap proxies to form an adaptive shortlist, (2) cache singleton HVs and use CELF lazy updates, (3) prune with box‑volume/singleton upper bounds and cap HV calls, (4) avoid Python O(N) membership in inner loops (use sets/indices), and (5) only pairwise‑cache when shortlist ≲40. Keep shortlist size adaptive to k and Pareto size.

Use an adaptive shortlist (k- and Pareto-aware), compute cheap HVC approximations (many weight vectors) to rank candidates, run CELF with cached singleton HVs + timestamps, prune with tight box-volume/upper bounds, enforce an HV-call budget, and do a capped candidate-limited deterministic swap pass using incremental HV deltas. This yields reproducible, high-quality subsets with predictable runtime.
comprehensive_reflection:
- Keywords: CELF, cached singleton HVC, adaptive shortlist, multi‑weight scalarization, box upper bounds, HV‑call budget, incremental HVC deltas, vectorization, deterministic seed.
- Advice: Prefilter dominated points, build an adaptive shortlist (≈c·k or Pareto‑aware) ranked by multi‑weight scalar HVC + box upper bounds, run CELF with cached singleton HVs and timestamps, limit exact pairwise HVCs to the small shortlist, do a capped deterministic 1‑for‑1 swap pass using incremental HVC updates, vectorize/reuse buffers, and enforce an HV‑call budget while returning exactly k.
- Avoid: Nested Python loops that trigger repeated HV recomputations, unbounded pairwise caching on large shortlists, nondeterministic seeds, and recomputing HVCs from scratch.
- Explanation: Cheap proxies + lazy/cached updates cut expensive HV calls; incremental/parallel HV checks and vectorization make quality predictable and reproducible.
